And that's where I see that becoming the success metric is like, yeah look what it's influencing, you know?
political_tussles.speaker: Daniel Henderson

political_tussles.tension_label: how to report quarterly pipeline numbers
political_tussles.summary: Uncertainty about whether to align to any-touch vs first-touch for quarterly reporting back to the business
political_tussles.quote: I guess this is just for the glean team is like how do we report back on our quarters? Like are we all aligning to any touch as like our pipeline? Yeah.
political_tussles.speaker: Daniel Henderson

political_tussles.summary: Field marketers being asked to justify events that don't generate net new pipeline, needing to show open pipeline influence instead
political_tussles.quote: the business actually is coming around to looking at influencer open pipeline influence metrics a little bit more just because, you know, we're seeing, we're seeing both a like decrease in like net new meetings and net new pipeline and more of a request on the field marketers to work on events that influence existing deals
political_tussles.speaker: 416 Gleanhog Day

political_tussles.summary: LinkedIn team making last-minute requests before QBR that may be for their own presentation polish rather than actual business insight, causing frustration
political_tussles.quote: I was hoping with the monthly calls we could kind of just go through with them like, okay, what are you being asked? What can we like reasonably triage? And so that we don't get into this situation where they're coming to us like the day before their QBR that like made me actually kind of angry that they're coming to us the day before their QBR for like all of these different metric asks that like, is this actually gonna move your presentation along or is it actually, is it just something that you want to know because you want LinkedIn to look really good to put it like very frankly.
political_tussles.speaker: 416 Gleanhog Day

political_tussles.tension_label: Sales working out of Salesforce vs marketing tools
political_tussles.summary: Sales team instructed to work out of Salesforce, creating tension about where RevSure data should live
political_tussles.quote: our sales team is instructed to work out of Salesforce for the most part so a lot of the other tools that we connect in there, usually they have an iframe or we create a new tab for them, or we push in the fields.
political_tussles.speaker: Weisi [Way-Sah]

political_tussles.summary: CEO doesn't understand branding, needs hard data to justify marketing spend increases
political_tussles.quote: my ceo is still having a tough time with like he's not a marketer he doesn't understand why branding works and how it works but they've got a lot of faith in digital from some of these stories that we've been given them so if we just like nail down that it's like showing the historical progression
political_tussles.speaker: Daniel Henderson

political_tussles.tension_label: budget reallocation based on conversion data
political_tussles.summary: Daniel found core target title doesn't convert to S2 well, pushing reallocation of LinkedIn budget to better-converting titles
political_tussles.quote: daniel had made an interesting um observation in LinkedIn where he was looking at like s1 to s2 conversion from LinkedIn by title and realize that like one of the title one of the core quote-unquote core titles that we target actually doesn't convert to s2 as well as some of the other ones so he's starting to think about reallocating budget and targeting towards some of the other titles that convert better
political_tussles.speaker: Weisi [Way-Sah]

political_tussles.summary: SDRs need real-time scoring to clear queues daily, but RevSure's daily sync creates friction
political_tussles.quote: our expectation for our inbound sales team and actually for our SDR team is that they clear their queues every single day, their inbound lead queues every single day. And so without, you know, understanding like what priority somebody is or what somebody has engaged with or what they should be doing with it. they need to be able to either like add it to the correct sequence or just take the right action and without knowing that in real time it gets hard to tell
political_tussles.speaker: Weisi [Way-Sah]

contrarians.claim: Skepticism that attribution tool can actually solve the problem - 'too good to be true'
contrarians.why_unusual: Most prospects express optimism; this buyer explicitly voices deep skepticism born from career-long failed attempts
contrarians.quote: Feels a little too good to be true, but excited that did DU sought out to solve this problem because there's so many marketers that are pulling their hair out trying to answer these questions
contrarians.speaker: Gleanff National Park

contrarians.claim: Propensity models shouldn't guide strategy for completely new markets because model won't know
contrarians.why_unusual: Buyers usually want AI to tell them where to go; this buyer correctly identifies the cold-start problem and limits of historical-data models
contrarians.quote: Use the propensity as a guide for which markets we should go after because the model won't know if we haven't already marketed to those people. You are, right?
contrarians.speaker: Gleanff National Park

contrarians.claim: Black box machine learning will create new political problems even while solving old ones
contrarians.why_unusual: Prospect anticipates and plans for new organizational friction from AI attribution, showing sophisticated change management thinking
contrarians.quote: the black box of the machine learning will, you know, is gonna be an issue a little bit. But you know, we'll have to get all our, our fricking buzzwords together and figure out how we can kind of come combat that, Hey Glen, how come my motion gets less attribution than this other 'cause that's what the box says
contrarians.speaker: Glen Shillinglaw

contrarians.claim: First-party cookies are not going away; only third-party cookies are, and the fear is overblown
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors hype cookieless future and fingerprinting as replacement; RevSure downplays urgency, saying first-party cookies persist
contrarians.quote: Actually the first party cookies cont are continuing across the board, right? Oh, they are cookies. Yeah, first party cookies actually. So there is this kind of fear about cookies, but it's actually only about third party cookies, not first party cookies. So if you as clean are collecting information about the customer visiting your website, it's a first party interaction for you. And that cookie is not going away, by the way, right? It's only the third party cookies.
contrarians.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

contrarians.claim: Enterprise DNA from Fortune 500 problem-solving beats SMB-tool evolution path
contrarians.why_unusual: Most SaaS vendors claim SMB origins and gradual upmarket movement as advantage; RevSure argues opposite - enterprise-first DNA is superior
contrarians.quote: we are not a solution that started doing SMB and website analytics and then we are now doing mid market and then we are going to do enterprise, right? That's not the origin of this company. The origin of this company is that, you know, I worked for the largest Fortune 500 companies in the world and I've seen all kinds of data variability, I've seen all kinds of scenarios, right? And that's how from scratch, it's not something that I'm evolving the architecture
contrarians.speaker: Deepinder Singh Dhingra

contrarians.claim: Attribution should show you LESS credit than platform-native reporting, and that's the hard truth marketers need
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors promise to validate marketers' self-reported numbers; RevSure emphasizes attribution will often show lower performance than native ad reports
contrarians.quote: What is still gets people really caught up, like gets really people caught in this kind of web is people have a hard time separating reporting an attribution like a really big time because they'll be like, oh, I, I am the digital marketer and I created 500 leads in Google, but you're telling me an attribution that I've only really created 35 and I only have two op real opportunities. It's like, well, yeah, but this is the, this is the reality. You know, like this is, but it's also like as much as that is a, a hard pill to swallow for some people, and that's like the like streak, right? Like it is the truth and it is the attribution
contrarians.speaker: Alex Cox

contrarians.claim: First-touch attribution is simple but misleading because buyers need many touchpoints before purchasing
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors sell first-touch as the gold standard; Harry Hawk argues simplicity doesn't equal validity and that needing 943 other touchpoints proves first touch isn't that valuable
contrarians.quote: Why is that, you know, why does that, why is that somehow better? 'cause the first thing that they did, if that first thing was so valuable, they would see that thing and they would be booked, you know, then I would believe that it really had value. But the fact that they need 943 other touch points before they buy says it's not really that valuable. It's, it's an, it's an interesting thing to talk about and, and, you know, to understand, but it's, it's not, you know, just 'cause it's simple doesn't mean that it's really helping you understand what's going on.
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: LinkedIn has strong impact on bookings but weak impact on pipeline generation - a counterintuitive finding
contrarians.why_unusual: Typically LinkedIn is measured on top-of-funnel pipeline; market mix modeling showed 57% booking impact but minimal pipeline impact, and specifically helps low-value accounts not high-value ones
contrarians.quote: What we're showing for pipeline generation volume, this blue is the impact of LinkedIn, which, which is not that great, but, you know, that's its average influence. Let me roof this down so you can see. Yeah. But when we look at booking volume, it's a 57% impact. So it is significantly impacting booking.
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: LinkedIn leads convert better but sales works them less, suggesting sales behavior not lead quality is the problem
contrarians.why_unusual: Typically marketing gets blamed for poor lead quality; here data shows sales prioritization is the issue despite better marketing performance
contrarians.quote: this is the classic issue that marketing faces is that you know, when sales does get a hot lead and they're working it, they tend to work their own leads harder
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: Events that don't generate first-touch pipeline may still be valuable for engaging existing pipeline
contrarians.why_unusual: Contradicts typical event ROI measurement focused on net new leads; reframes event value toward acceleration
contrarians.quote: this event has turned into, maybe it is turning into something where we engage with existing customers and get further into an account versus sourcing net new
contrarians.speaker: 416 Gleanhog Day

contrarians.claim: Blog posts may be 'moment in time' content that should be scaled back versus evergreen product pages
contrarians.why_unusual: Content teams typically push for more blog output; data showed product pages got more sustained engagement
contrarians.quote: maybe we could find a way to scale back the blogs because they're kind of like a moment in time thing. We see it in the page visits, we see it in like how long people are reading them, but then we also see them in, you know, how they're moving people along in sales cycle
contrarians.speaker: 416 Gleanhog Day

contrarians.claim: LinkedIn reps don't actually know how to use RevSure despite presenting as experts to customers
contrarians.why_unusual: Typically vendor partnerships imply mutual product expertise; admitting LinkedIn reps are untrained on the tool they recommend is unusual transparency
contrarians.quote: I also didn't realize that LinkedIn didn't know the platform as well as I thought that they did. They made it seem like they, they knew it or that they had existing relationships with you. And so like that clearly is not the case.
contrarians.speaker: 416 Gleanhog Day

contrarians.claim: Spending 10x more on Google than LinkedIn but getting same long-term user contribution
contrarians.why_unusual: Contradicts typical last-touch attribution that would heavily favor Google; shows value of multi-touch modeling for brand channels
contrarians.quote: even though there was this 10 to one dispar even know difference in spend, they both almost contributed the same amount towards long-term users, which in this case was like 20% versus seven 17 or 18%.
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: MMX is a counterfactual to attribution, not a replacement — you need both for a 360-degree view, and where they disagree is equally important as where they agree
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors position their solution as the single source of truth; RevSure explicitly says you need four different models (lift test, FCA, MMX, DGE) and should arbitrage between them
contrarians.quote: when you combine lift test fca and mmx along with whatever you're doing in dge you've got kind of a 360 degree view you're going to have different opinions that you're going to have to arbitrage and take position on but you're going to have four really good um positions and then obviously where they do agree that's interesting and but i think it's equally important where they disagree right
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: Traditional attribution's cause-and-effect logic is fundamentally limited compared to multivariable impact modeling
contrarians.why_unusual: While many vendors critique last-touch, explicitly calling out that even multi-touch attribution misses cross-channel incremental effects is less common; the BirdEye example of Facebook driving 5% compounded branded search growth for 10-15 weeks after being turned off illustrates this dramatically
contrarians.quote: in traditional attribution, you know, it's very cause and effect, right? Someone clicked on an ad, they filled out a form. and we're attributing, you know, okay, well, they clicked on the ad, so we're giving credit to that ad, right? Or multi-touch, we consider several touch points, where MMX is looking at not only the statistics of how all of these impact, but it's also looking at the multivariable impact, meaning we know at BirdEye, we did a test They had Facebook off, they turned it on, spent a quarter million dollars in 15 days. And then for the next 10 to 15 weeks, 5% compounded week over week growth in branded search.
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: MMX predictions are fixed at quarter start and NOT updated — deliberately not real-time
contrarians.why_unusual: Most SaaS analytics dashboards emphasize real-time or near-real-time updates; explaining that the model is intentionally static until next quarter is counterintuitive and requires client education
contrarians.quote: the part that to me that's important to understand because we thought we when i first saw that we thought well let's come back next week and see how it's changed but it's not updated until the beginning of the quarter so it's based on all of last quarter's results
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: LinkedIn is outperforming Google Ads significantly, contrary to typical B2B performance patterns
contrarians.why_unusual: Google typically dominates B2B search advertising; LinkedIn is usually considered expensive and lower funnel
contrarians.quote: like the first job I've had where LinkedIn is outshining Google ads and so it's been very eye-opening how much linkedin has progressed in their advertising platform
contrarians.speaker: Daniel Henderson

contrarians.claim: Would double down on LinkedIn even with diminishing returns because volume is better than Google's ROI
contrarians.why_unusual: Contradicts typical marketing efficiency optimization; explicitly prioritizes growth over ROI in hyper-growth context
contrarians.quote: i'd say is like linkedin i'd double down into that even if it was telling me diminishing returns because the volume it's getting is a lot better than google ads right now
contrarians.speaker: Daniel Henderson

contrarians.claim: Unopened emails have influence value
contrarians.why_unusual: Most attribution models require engagement; claiming unopened emails influence is non-standard
contrarians.quote: i'm a believer that email even unopened email has influence
contrarians.speaker: Harry Hawk

contrarians.claim: First-touch pipeline makes strategic event and field marketing look bad, hiding their true value in influence
contrarians.why_unusual: Many orgs still use first-touch as primary metric; explicit rejection for events is notable
contrarians.quote: being more flexible with the strategic event and field marketing budgets to start investing more in events that they know will influence existing pipeline or help customer renewal and upsell versus just generating net new
contrarians.speaker: Weisi [Way-Sah]

contrarians.claim: Used to hate branding but figured out how to tie it to demand gen and pipeline
contrarians.why_unusual: Most marketers defend branding instinctively; openly admitting past hatred and finding data-driven bridge to demand gen is rare
contrarians.quote: I used to hate branding, but we've been able to figure out how this ties to like demand gen and pipeline
contrarians.speaker: Daniel Henderson

contrarians.claim: Completion rate (7% to 23%) is more informative than raw lead volume for hyper-growth decisions
contrarians.why_unusual: Most growth-focused teams obsess over top-of-funnel volume; prioritizing completion rate over raw area/volume goes against typical hyper-growth playbook
contrarians.quote: that's where that KPI that I'm looking at seems more informative than this raw area when we're just trying to go for the hyper growth
contrarians.speaker: Daniel Henderson

contrarians.claim: Cold audience getting better and warm getting worse over time - counterintuitive progression
contrarians.why_unusual: Conventional wisdom is warm audiences convert better; seeing cold improve while warm declines challenges typical funnel assumptions
contrarians.quote: the cold had gone from seven percent to twenty three percent and so that was like shocking that i was like man after doing this for so long this cold is getting better and warm i guess
contrarians.speaker: Daniel Henderson

contrarians.claim: Out-of-home took 5 months to show lead impact, used that delay to justify MORE brand spend
contrarians.why_unusual: Most orgs would kill delayed-ROI channels; Glean used the data to justify expansion to London and US Open
contrarians.quote: it took like basically five months for us to see any sort of like movement or impact on even just the leads created. We shared that with our CMO and he and that was kind of helpful in justifying additional brand spend for another like city swarm type activation
contrarians.speaker: Weisi [Way-Sah]

contrarians.claim: AI agents should NOT be given to every marketer because they lack context to interpret outputs correctly
contrarians.why_unusual: Most vendors push democratization of AI; Weisi argues for restricted access due to context requirements
contrarians.quote: what i wouldn't want to do is give this to like every single marketer and be like okay now you can ask this ai questions about all of our data and then risk it like the reason that this works for somebody like me is because I have context about the data that we're looking at and what the filters mean and how everything fits together. Whereas somebody else can just take this at face value and be like, well, this is the answer, like, and not know how to double check.
contrarians.speaker: Weisi [Way-Sah]

contrarians.claim: First-touch attribution actively misleads about content value - gated content looks worse than it is
contrarians.why_unusual: While multi-touch attribution is common, explicitly calling first-touch 'misleading' for content strategy is a stronger stance than typical
contrarians.quote: when we looked at just the 1st touch pipeline. for all of our gated content compared with the pre and post influence. There was a ton of other content that was influencing, you know, influencing deals or even playing a bigger role in the actual deal cycle itself that was not showing up in first touch pipeline.
contrarians.speaker: Weisi [Way-Sah]

contrarians.claim: Moving to S2 as canonical pipeline definition rather than S1
contrarians.why_unusual: Most orgs define pipeline at first stage (S1/SQL); pushing to S2 as 'true pipeline' is a stricter standard
contrarians.quote: Our pipeline, what we're calling pipeline, like canonically pipeline is going to be S2 instead of S1
contrarians.speaker: Weisi [Way-Sah]
